notes: Twenty-seventh validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its public-facing motto is the three-part line: Educate. Motivate. Graduate.
The school's mission says every student should receive a quality and relevant education tailored to background, learning style, and future pathway.
Official materials say its vision is to graduate productive citizens ready for lifelong learning and careers in a global society.
The homepage says scholars are empowered to discover their passions, choose their path, and define their future.
Official pages highlight AP Capstone as a signature academic offering.
Recent staff materials list Daryl Woodard as Head of School.
Its main about page uses the line: A School of Choice. A Culture of Belonging.
Official materials describe it as the first tuition-free, elementary STEM-based public charter school in Wayne County.
The school says it avoids one-size-fits-all instruction by using Direct Instruction and mastery-based placement.
Its published core charter values include responsibility, respect, resilience, diversity, courage, and industriousness.
Official leadership pages list Dr. Todd Forgette as Executive Director.
Governance materials identify Christina Watts as board chair.
Its mission says the school develops responsible and active citizens through a challenging classical academic program, character education, student responsibility, and strong parental partnerships.
A public message from its founding year says the school draws from both the Core Knowledge Sequence and the classical education tradition.
Official curriculum materials say students are introduced to Latin and Logic as part of the academic program.
The school says its four pillars are rigorous academics, parental partnerships, character education, and student responsibility.
Official board materials list John O'Day as chairman.
Public leadership messages identify Dr. Jarrid Looney as the founding Head of School.
Its motto is the line, "We Raise the Quality of Life of Everyone We Touch."
Official materials say the school prides itself on teaching scholars to be proud of their indigenous culture.
Its mission says the school aims to maximize students' educational development while aligning costs with major districts in North Carolina and its home district.
The school's vision is to create a sustainable educational organization that develops a replicable, self-sufficient, wholesome community.
Official leadership pages identify Dr. Tamara M. Turner as CEO.
Board materials list Dr. Jefferson Caruthers, Jr. as chairman.
The school regularly refers to its students as Rockets.
Its mission says the school promotes high academic achievement by increasing student engagement through a workshop model.
Official materials say the curriculum is designed around hands-on STEM experiences that build critical thinking and problem-solving.
The school highlights project-based learning as one of its core selling points for families.
Administrative pages list Teddy McIlwain as principal.
Recent school announcements say it earned full accreditation with STEM Certification from Cognia.
